history.historicalImportance: Aurangabad has been an important historical region of the Magadh area. It is known for its association with ancient Magadha, the medieval rulers of Bihar, and the freedom movement. The district is also famous for the Sun Temple at Deo, one of the most important Sun temples in India.
history.formationHistory: Aurangabad district was carved out of the erstwhile Gaya district and officially came into existence on 26 January 1973.
history.freedomMovement: The people of Aurangabad actively participated in the Indian freedom movement. Many local freedom fighters joined the Non-Cooperation Movement, Civil Disobedience Movement, and Quit India Movement against British rule.
history.archaeologicalImportance: The district contains several historical and archaeological sites, including the famous Deo Sun Temple, ancient inscriptions, old temples, forts, and medieval structures reflecting the rich cultural heritage of Magadh.
historicalTimeline: Ancient period – The region formed part of the Magadha Mahajanapada.
Mauryan period – Came under the rule of the Maurya Empire.
Gupta period – Flourished as part of the Gupta Empire.
Medieval period – Ruled by the Delhi Sultanate and later the Mughal Empire.
1765 – Came under the administration of the British East India Company.
26 January 1973 – Aurangabad became a separate district after being carved out of Gaya district.

geography.location: Aurangabad district is located in the south-western part of Bihar in the Magadh Division. It lies on the Chota Nagpur Plateau fringe and forms an important gateway between Bihar and Jharkhand.
geography.climate: Subtropical climate with hot summers, monsoon rainfall and cool winters.
geography.forests: Moderate forest cover, mainly in the southern region

specialEconomicFeatures: NTPC Nabinagar Super Thermal Power Project
Fertile plains of the Son River basin
Stone mining and stone crushing industries
Strategic location on the Grand Trunk Road (NH-19)

agriculture.dairyDevelopment: Dairy farming is an important source of income, supported by milk cooperative societies and private dairy collection centres.
agriculture.fisheries: Fish farming is practiced in ponds, reservoirs and village water bodies across the district.

infrastructure.roadNetwork: Aurangabad district has a well-developed road network connecting all major towns and blocks through National Highways, State Highways and district roads.
infrastructure.airConnectivity: The nearest airport is Gaya International Airport. Jay Prakash Narayan International Airport, Patna, also provides air connectivity.
infrastructure.publicTransport: Bihar State Road Transport Corporation (BSRTC) buses, private buses, taxis and auto-rickshaws provide public transport services across the district.
infrastructure.electricity: Most villages and urban areas have electricity connectivity, supported by the South Bihar Power Distribution Company Limited (SBPDCL).
infrastructure.waterSupply: Urban areas receive piped water supply, while rural areas are served through hand pumps, tube wells and government water supply schemes.
infrastructure.internetConnectivity: 4G and 5G mobile networks are available in most parts of the district, along with broadband internet services.

education.governmentSchools: More than 1,500 government primary, middle and secondary schools operate across the district.
education.privateSchools: Several CBSE, ICSE and Bihar Board affiliated private schools are located in Aurangabad and other towns.
universities: No university is located in Aurangabad district. Most colleges are affiliated with Magadh University, Bodh Gaya.
medicalColleges: No government medical college in the district

healthcare.primaryHealthCentres: Primary Health Centres (PHCs) are functioning in all blocks of the district.
medicalColleges: No government medical college in the district
healthcare.ambulanceServices: 102 and 108 ambulance services are available throughout the district.

cultureAndHeritage.traditionalDress: Men traditionally wear Dhoti-Kurta and women wear Saree, especially Tussar and Cotton Sarees during festivals and cultural occasions.
historicalHeritage: Ancient temples of the Magadh region
Historical sites associated with the Son River valley
culturalOrganizations: District Art and Culture Office, Aurangabad
Department of Art, Culture and Youth, Bihar

historicalSites: Ancient monuments of the Magadh region
wildlifeSanctuaries: No wildlife sanctuary within the district

sportsAchievements: District athletes regularly participate in state-level competitions.
School and college teams represent Aurangabad in Bihar-level tournaments.

bankingAndFinance.atms: ATMs of major public sector and private sector banks are available across the district.
bankingAndFinance.digitalBanking: UPI, Internet Banking, Mobile Banking, AEPS and Bharat Bill Payment System (BBPS) services are widely available.
financialInclusionPrograms: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana

governanceAndPublicServices.districtAdministration: The district is administered by the District Magistrate (DM), Aurangabad, under the Government of Bihar.
governanceAndPublicServices.policeAdministration: Law and order is maintained by Bihar Police under the Aurangabad District Police administration.
governanceAndPublicServices.fireServices: Fire stations operate in Aurangabad, Daudnagar and other major towns of the district.
governanceAndPublicServices.publicDistributionSystem: Essential commodities are distributed through Fair Price Shops (FPS) under the Public Distribution System (PDS).
eGovernanceServices: Bihar Right to Public Services Portal

lawAndOrder.policeDistrict: Aurangabad Police District under Bihar Police
lawAndOrder.trafficPolice: Traffic Police regulates traffic and road safety across Aurangabad district.
lawAndOrder.disasterManagement: The District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA), Aurangabad, coordinates disaster preparedness, relief and rehabilitation activities.

environment.forestCover: Moderate forest cover, mainly in the southern part of the district.

mediaAndCommunication.postalServices: India Post provides postal, Speed Post and parcel services through Head Post Office, Sub Post Offices and Branch Post Offices across the district.
mediaAndCommunication.telecommunication: 4G and 5G mobile connectivity is available in most parts of the district, while broadband and BharatNet services are expanding in rural areas.

stateRankings: One of the major districts of the Magadh region
Known for the famous Deo Sun Temple
Important agricultural district of South Bihar
nationalRankings: Recognized for the historic Deo Chhath Festival
Home to one of India's oldest Sun Temples
notableAchievements: Home to the internationally known Deo Sun Temple
NTPC Nabinagar Super Thermal Power Project has boosted industrial development
Growing road, power and digital infrastructure
developmentIndicators: Growth in electricity generation through NTPC Nabinagar
records: Deo Sun Temple is one of the oldest and most significant Sun Temples in India.
Deo Chhath Mela is one of the largest religious fairs in Bihar.

nicknames: Land of the Deo Sun Temple
importantDates: 26 January 1973 – Aurangabad became a separate district
15 August 1947 – Became part of independent India
additionalInformation: Aurangabad is internationally known for the Deo Sun Temple and the annual Deo Chhath Mela.
NTPC Nabinagar Super Thermal Power Project is one of the district's major industrial landmarks.
Agriculture remains the primary occupation of the district.
The Son River plays an important role in irrigation and the local economy.
